title: OpenAI opt-in cloud adapter contract and safe local env handling
problem: The repo has a spec-only OpenAI synthesis adapter packet, but ticket-059 is still a placeholder with empty expected_files and placeholder acceptance criteria. Builder/autocycle handoff needs a scoped, mock-first implementation ticket for an optional OpenAI cloud synthesis adapter with fail-closed env gates, safe .env.local key handling, provider allowlist validation, budget caps, circuit-breaker compatibility, and no default live calls.
acceptance_criteria: ticket-059 remains mock-first: default config, pytest, golden tests, operator execute-safe, fixture-mode runs, and release-governor inspect paths do not make live OpenAI or other paid provider calls.

OpenAI provider registration exists behind an explicit provider id such as openai and is selected only when the cloud synthesis path explicitly requests it; unknown or non-allowlisted providers fail closed.

A thin OpenAI synthesis adapter implements the evidence-packet-only contract: input contains grounded atoms, claim ids, source refs, and trace refs only; no raw prompts, raw source text, local paths, private notes, or secrets are sent or serialized.

OpenAI output is treated as candidate synthesis only and is validated by deterministic Python gates before any downstream artifact is written; model output never writes directly to accepted graph tables.

.env.local is loaded only through the repo's safe operator env-loading path, preferably by extending rge/modules/operator_env_loader.py as the operator wrapper over existing config loading; direct ad hoc dotenv parsing is not introduced.
.env.local remains gitignored and is blocked from patch bundles, release batches, public artifacts, reports, logs, prompts, Atlas JSON, and any serialized synthesis output.
The implementation never prints, logs, stores, or exports the API key value. Key checks may report only boolean availability, for example openai_key_available true or false.

Any live OpenAI HTTP call requires all gates: explicit provider allowlist, explicit live/cloud opt-in env flag, RGE_CLOUD_MAX_USD_PER_RUN, RGE_CLOUD_MAX_TOKENS_PER_CALL, closed autonomy circuit breaker, and successful mock-mode tests.
Budget validation rejects missing, nonnumeric, zero, or exceeded RGE_CLOUD_MAX_USD_PER_RUN and RGE_CLOUD_MAX_TOKENS_PER_CALL values before any live HTTP request is attempted.

Release governor compatibility is preserved: inspect and dry-run surfaces remain public-safe, .env.local and credential fields are never included in release batches, and irreversible release actions are not performed by this ticket.

Tests prove missing gates fail closed, mock adapter behavior is deterministic, key availability is redacted to a boolean, allowlist/cost/circuit gates block live calls, and the public-site/operator env guard still prevents public code from reading operator secrets.
Optional live smoke documentation or scaffold is allowed only behind explicit operator env gates, small budget caps, and a command that is excluded from default pytest and CI.
test_plan: Optional operator-only smoke, not CI: set explicit cloud/live gates, provider allowlist, RGE_CLOUD_MAX_USD_PER_RUN, RGE_CLOUD_MAX_TOKENS_PER_CALL, and a closed circuit breaker before running the documented smoke command.
safety_notes: Do not read, print, copy, commit, or serialize .env.local or any secret value.
Do not make live OpenAI calls by default; mock mode is the default for tests, CI, fixture paths, operator execute-safe, and normal development.
Provider responses are untrusted candidate JSON/prose and require deterministic schema, grounding, citation, private-field, and cost validation before downstream use.
No model output may write directly to accepted DB tables, change source credibility, approve safety results, activate domains/concepts, or trigger shell/Git/browser actions.
Public export and Atlas artifacts must contain only public-safe fields and must never include provider keys, raw prompts, raw source text, local paths, private notes, or .env.local-derived values.

required_for_any_live_openai_call: OPENAI_API_KEY is present but never printed
autonomy circuit breaker status is closed
forbidden_defaults: No live HTTP in default pytest, golden tests, fixture-mode runs, execute-safe, or release governor inspect.
No silent fallback from failed live OpenAI calls to mock in live mode.
No cloud mode activation from .env.example defaults.

cloud_cost_caps.policy: Validate caps before request construction, record only public-safe cost/usage summaries, and fail closed if usage would exceed the configured cap.

env_local_handling: .env.local is operator-local and gitignored; it must not appear in expected patch bundles, release batches, public artifacts, reports, logs, prompts, Atlas JSON, or serialized model inputs/outputs.

Load .env.local only through the existing safe env-loading path. Prefer extending rge/modules/operator_env_loader.py as the operator-facing wrapper over rge/config.py instead of introducing independent dotenv parsing.
Never inspect or print the OPENAI_API_KEY value. If availability is reported, expose only openai_key_available true or false.
Process environment variables may override local file values, but reporting remains redacted and boolean-only for credentials.
non_goals: Do not implement OpenRouter or other provider adapters in this ticket.
Do not make live OpenAI calls as part of normal tests, golden tests, execute-safe, fixture runs, or release-governor inspect.
Do not require a real API key for unit, golden, verify, or safety tests.
Do not weaken release governor, autonomy circuit breaker, public export policy, safety auditor, or patch-staging gates.
Do not write model output directly to accepted graph tables.
Do not add public write routes, public source ingestion routes, public agent execution routes, or public cloud-model endpoints.
Do not mutate synthesis governor ledger rows, historical remediation rows, accepted research data, public export snapshots, or unrelated tickets.
Do not edit TICKET_QUEUE.md unless a separate human-reviewed queue-status action requires it.
Do not perform remote Git, mainline integration, public-deploy, queue-advancement, or release actions.
rollback_plan: Disable cloud mode by leaving RGE_CLOUD_LLM_ENABLED=0 and removing openai from the provider allowlist, then revert the OpenAI adapter, env-loader, registry, docs, and tests changed by this ticket. Because the adapter must not change DB schema or public export contracts, rollback should not require data migration.
mock_first_behavior: RGE_LLM_MODE=mock remains the default in .env.example and all automated tests.
Mock synthesis tests must prove behavior without OPENAI_API_KEY.
Live smoke, if added, must be skipped by default and require explicit operator env gates plus cost caps.
live_call_opt_in_rules: No live HTTP request may be constructed unless provider allowlist, cloud/live env gates, cost caps, and circuit breaker checks pass.
The command must show only public-safe status such as provider id, budget cap values, estimated cost, token cap, circuit status, and openai_key_available true or false.
The command must not serialize raw request prompts, raw source text, .env.local contents, local paths, or credential values.
